artifacts.content:
From: laura.henderson.properties@gmail.com
To: michael.henderson@gmail.com
Subject: HELOC draw for camera bid – please review
Date: Thu, 3 Oct 2024 9:14 PM

Hey hon,

I finally got the full quote back from Inland Security Systems for the upgraded cameras and lighting at Banning Plaza. I’m attaching the PDF they sent over this afternoon.

The bad news: it’s more than we hoped. Between the new motion-activated LED fixtures for the rear alley, replacing the two ripped-down cameras by the dumpsters, and adding the extra coverage along the loading bay behind the grocery store, their total comes to $46,800, not including any surprises once they open up the walls.

Given how much we’ve already spent this year on emergency cleanups and repairs, I don’t see a way to do this out of regular cash flow. Since we opened that $125,000 home equity line of credit with Pacific Horizon Credit Union here in Beaumont in September, this is exactly the kind of thing I was afraid of. By my count we’ve already drawn a little over $80,000 for the vandalism repairs, the temporary security patrols, and just keeping the plaza afloat during those months of reduced rent.

I hate that the house is basically on the line now between this and Emily’s tuition at UC Riverside, but I also don’t see how we can ignore the security issues and keep tenants. After the customer tripped on broken glass near the dumpsters on 11/02 and ended up at San Gorgonio for stitches, I feel like we’re one bad night away from a full-blown lawsuit.

Can you look through the Inland bid and tell me what you think before I sign anything? Maybe you can sanity-check their electrical labor since you actually understand that part, unlike me.

Jacob’s game is at 6:30 tomorrow, so I’ll be home by 5:30 if nothing at the plaza blows up. Let’s talk then.

Love,
Laura

metadata.subject: HELOC draw for camera bid – please review

artifacts.content:
From: laura.henderson.properties@gmail.com
To: maria.ramseynails@gmail.com
Subject: Re: Notice of Security Concerns and Potential Lease Termination
Date: Tue, 20 Aug 2024 8:02 AM

Hi Maria,

Thank you for the letter you hand-delivered yesterday, "Notice of Security Concerns and Potential Lease Termination." I’ve read it several times and I’m truly sorry you and your customers have been going through this.

I take seriously what you wrote about possibly consulting an attorney about constructive eviction if conditions at Banning Plaza don’t improve by the end of the year. I know suite 104 depends heavily on walk-in traffic, and when groups are loitering, drinking, or blocking your doorway, it’s not only intimidating but directly hurts your business.

Since May, I’ve been trying to push every lever I can: more frequent calls to Banning PD, hiring extra overnight patrols when the budget allows, and getting bids for brighter lighting and more durable cameras. You know about the May 18 incident around 12:45 a.m. when I called the night shift and Sgt. Daniel Ruiz basically told me, “We can’t keep sending units every time someone is sleeping in your doorway.” I was frustrated enough that on May 21 I emailed the department’s community liaison with the subject line “Concerns re: Sgt. Ruiz response 5/18/24.”

I’m not sharing that to make excuses, just to say I’m not shrugging this off. I’ve also been working with La Familia Grill at the end cap; their evening business is down almost 30% because of aggressive panhandling near their entrance. In October I’m planning a temporary rent adjustment to help them stay afloat while we get these security upgrades in place.

Can we sit down this Thursday at 10:00 a.m. in your shop to walk through specific problem times and locations so I can prioritize better? I want Ramsey Nails to stay in suite 104 for many years, and I’m committed to making real changes.

Best,
Laura

metadata.subject: Re: Notice of Security Concerns and Potential Lease Termination

artifacts.content:
From: laura.henderson.properties@gmail.com
To: james.porter@porter-kleinlaw.com
Subject: Re: Incident of 11/02/2024 – Notice of Representation and Demand for Preservation of Evidence
Date: Tue, 19 Nov 2024 2:37 PM

Dear Mr. Porter,

I acknowledge receipt of your November 18, 2024 email regarding the incident of 11/02/2024 at Banning Plaza Retail Center, subject line “Re: Incident of 11/02/2024 – Notice of Representation and Demand for Preservation of Evidence.”

First and foremost, I am very sorry that your client was injured after tripping on broken glass near the dumpsters behind the center. As the managing owner of the property at 1450 W Ramsey St in Banning, I take the safety of customers and tenants extremely seriously.

Per your request, I am taking immediate steps to preserve all potentially relevant evidence, including:

- Downloading and backing up security camera footage from the rear alley and dumpster area for November 1–3, 2024.
- Preserving maintenance logs and work orders related to trash pickup, glass cleanup, and vandalism in that area.
- Retaining copies of any incident reports or internal emails referencing the 11/02/2024 injury.

I have already notified our insurance carrier and forwarded your letter to them so they can open a claim file. Future correspondence regarding liability and any settlement discussions will likely come through the adjuster, but I will continue to cooperate fully.

For context only (not as an excuse), we have been dealing with escalating vandalism, illegal lodging, and littering near the dumpsters for over a year now. My landscaper recently found a makeshift fire pit built from broken concrete blocks in the same area, and we’ve had to hire professional cleaners twice in the last month to sanitize human waste behind the grocery store. I am in the process of contracting for upgraded lighting and cameras and coordinating more closely with neighboring property owners along Ramsey Street to reduce these hazards.

Please let me know if you need any additional documentation from my side at this stage.

Sincerely,

Laura M. Henderson
Managing Owner, Henderson Commercial Properties LLC
Banning Plaza Retail Center
1450 W Ramsey St, Banning, CA 92220
Phone: (951) 555-2378

metadata.subject: Re: Incident of 11/02/2024 – Notice of Representation and Demand for Preservation of Evidence

artifacts.content:
From: laura.henderson.properties@gmail.com
To: lafamiliagrill@gmail.com
Subject: Temporary Rent Adjustment – Please Keep Confidential
Date: Mon, 7 Oct 2024 11:06 AM

Hi Carlos and Ana,

Thank you again for meeting with me last week to talk about how things have been going at La Familia Grill. I’ve been thinking a lot about what you shared regarding the drop in your evening business.

I know you estimated roughly a 30% loss in dinner customers over the last few months, especially on weekends, because of the groups hanging out near your front entrance, panhandling, drinking, and sometimes arguing loudly in the parking lot. I’ve seen some of it myself when I do evening drive-throughs.

Until we get the new lighting and cameras installed and (hopefully) a more consistent police presence, I don’t want you to feel like you’re carrying this burden alone. So for October and November 2024, I’m willing to accept half of your usual base rent as a temporary adjustment.

I’m putting this in writing here so we’re all clear:

- October 2024: 50% of base rent due by the 10th
- November 2024: 50% of base rent due by the 10th
- CAM charges and utilities remain unchanged and due as usual

Subject line says it, but I’ll repeat: please keep this confidential between us. If other tenants hear that I’ve made a special arrangement, it will open the door to a lot of difficult conversations, and the plaza’s finances are already tight. Michael and I actually had to open a home equity line of credit with Pacific Horizon Credit Union in Beaumont last month just to keep up with security costs, repairs, and still cover Emily’s tuition at UC Riverside.

My hope is that by December we’ll see improvement and can revisit for December’s rent and beyond.

Thank you for sticking with Banning Plaza through all of this.

Best,
Laura

metadata.subject: Temporary Rent Adjustment – Please Keep Confidential

artifacts.content:
From: laura.henderson.properties@gmail.com
To: karen.miller@gmail.com
Subject: Catch-up: Mom, work, and everything else
Date: Sun, 15 Sep 2024 9:42 PM

Hey sis,

Finally sitting down after Jacob’s tournament and a Costco run. Figured I’d write instead of just sending a random meme.

Mom’s still mad about the driving. She brought up that March 14, 2023 fender-bender at the Hemet Target again, saying it was “just a bump” and that Dr. Liu at Hemet Valley Medical Center is overreacting. She actually texted me the other day, “Dr. Liu is overreacting, I can still drive to church,” in all caps. I reminded her that the geriatric clinic told her to stop driving because of the balance issues, but she thinks I’m siding with the doctor against her. No wonder I’m burning so many Saturdays driving to Hemet for errands.

Work isn’t helping my stress. The loitering and illegal lodging at Banning Plaza keep getting worse. On May 18 around 12:45 a.m., I was on the phone with a Banning PD night-shift sergeant, Sgt. Ruiz, about people sleeping in front of the restaurant, and he actually said, “We can’t keep sending units every time someone is sleeping in your doorway.” I was so upset I fired off an email to their community liaison on May 21 titled “Concerns re: Sgt. Ruiz response 5/18/24.” Not sure it did any good, but at least it’s on record.

Between the late-night tenant calls, worrying about someone starting a fire behind the center, and dealing with Mom’s stubbornness, I finally gave in and started seeing a therapist in June. Her name is Dr. Elena Chavez at Riverside Behavioral Health Center. I go every other Wednesday at 8:00 a.m. My first MyChart reminder in June literally said “New Patient Visit – Dr. Chavez (Anxiety/Stress).” Kind of surreal to see it spelled out like that.

Anyway, enough doom and gloom. How are the kids? Send me pictures from their first week of school.

Love,
Laura

metadata.subject: Catch-up: Mom, work, and everything else

artifacts.content:
From: laura.henderson.properties@gmail.com
To: me
Subject: Note to self – plaza stress + Emily
Date: Tue, 9 Jul 2024 11:58 PM

Just need to get this out of my head.

Tonight at the kitchen table Emily and I had that awful conversation I’ve been dreading. After you and I went over the numbers last week – the vandalism repairs, security patrols, the new camera bids – it was obvious we couldn’t afford on-campus housing at UC Riverside for 2024–2025. So I told her again that she has to commute from Beaumont.

She looked at me and said, through tears, “You’re choosing that plaza over my college experience.” It felt like she punched me. I tried to explain about the rising costs at Banning Plaza, how the loitering and aggressive panhandling are scaring off customers, how we’ve had to call professional cleaners twice in a month to deal with human waste behind the grocery store, and how we’re already behind because of the attempted break-ins on the vacant units and the broken camera housings.

I didn’t tell her that Michael and I are seriously considering a home equity line of credit just to cover security upgrades and her tuition. I’m scared if I say that out loud it will make it too real.

All of this is sitting on top of the late-night calls from tenants about people sleeping in front of their doors, plus the November 2023 near-miss when someone built a little fire pit out of concrete chunks by the back wall. I lie awake thinking about one spark hitting that dry brush.

Reminder to self: next appointment with Dr. Chavez at Riverside Behavioral Health Center is Wednesday 7/10 at 8:00 a.m. I need to talk about the guilt over Emily and whether I’m really “choosing the plaza” or just trying to keep us from going under.

Breathe.

– L

metadata.subject: Note to self – plaza stress + Emily

artifacts.content:
Event: Therapy – Dr. Elena Chavez (Anxiety/Stress)
Date: Wed, 12 Jun 2024
Time: 8:00–9:00 AM
Location: Riverside Behavioral Health Center – Dr. Chavez’s office
Attendees: Laura Henderson

Notes:

First visit with Dr. Elena Chavez about the anxiety and insomnia that have been getting worse since all the late-night tenant calls and security issues at Banning Plaza.

Things to mention:

- How often I’m waking up to calls/texts from tenants about people sleeping in front of their doors, drinking, shouting, etc.
- The May 18, 2024 12:45 a.m. call with Banning PD night-shift Sgt. Ruiz where he said, “We can’t keep sending units every time someone is sleeping in your doorway,” and how helpless/angry that made me feel.
- The increasing vandalism and the November 2 broken-glass injury near the dumpsters that led to the demand letter from James Porter at Porter & Klein LLP (“Re: Incident of 11/02/2024 – Notice of Representation and Demand for Preservation of Evidence” on 11/18/24) – constant fear of being sued.
- The pressure of considering a home equity line of credit at Pacific Horizon Credit Union in Beaumont to pay for security upgrades and still cover Emily’s UC Riverside tuition.
- Mom’s situation: Dr. Liu at Hemet Valley Medical Center telling her after the March 14, 2023 Hemet Target parking lot collision that she shouldn’t drive anymore, Mom texting “Dr. Liu is overreacting, I can still drive to church,” and how that’s added extra caregiving trips and guilt.
- Emily saying tonight, “You’re choosing that plaza over my college experience,” when we told her she has to commute instead of living on campus.

Goal: figure out coping strategies and whether medication is worth discussing if sleep doesn’t improve.

Hi everyone,

I hope this email finds you well as we wrap up 2024! I wanted to share some updates and plans for the upcoming year with you all, especially since it's been a busy time for our family.

Firstly, the past year at Banning Plaza has been challenging but rewarding. We've dealt with several issues, like loitering and vandalism, but I'm optimistic about 2025. I'm currently looking into enhanced security measures like brighter, motion-activated LEDs and more durable camera systems. I'm also in talks with neighboring property owners about a joint approach to security and trash pickup, which I believe will help alleviate some of the stress.

On a personal note, Michael and I are doing well. We've been married for 22 years now, and he's been a rock through all the late-night calls and unexpected trips to the plaza. In case of emergencies, you can always reach him at (951) 555-9184. Jacob is keeping us on our toes with his JV basketball games, and I try to make it to as many as I can, even if it means showing up in work clothes! Emily is thriving at UC Riverside and is home most weekends, which we cherish.

As we step into 2025, I've set some goals for both my business and personal life. I'm considering managing another property closer to Beaumont and possibly hiring a part-time assistant. This should hopefully free up some evenings and reduce the 6 a.m. starts after a late-night call.

For your records, our contact details remain the same: Mailing address is P.O. Box 783, Beaumont, CA 92223. My direct line is still (951) 555-2378, and Michael's is as mentioned above.

The upcoming year for Henderson Commercial Properties LLC officially begins on January 1, 2025, and will continue through December 31, 2025. I’m ready to tackle anything that comes our way! As always, your support means the world to us, and I’m grateful for each of you.

Wishing you all a joyful holiday season and a fantastic start to the new year. Let's make 2025 a great one!

Warm regards,

Laura M. Henderson
Date: 2024-12-11
